events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise
events.description: Ongoing strikes across the Middle East stemming from the US-Israeli bombing of Iran and subsequent counterstrikes in the Gulf by Tehran continue to drive humanitarian needs, civilian casualties and mass displacement. Concerns are also growing over maritime security, the economic crisis centred around the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, attacks on healthcare and the protection of critical infrastructure, including nuclear facilities. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 18 March: Lebanon displacement tops one million as nuclear safety concerns raised
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with ongoing strikes and rising humanitarian needs. The UN nuclear watchdog reported Wednesday that a projectile struck the site of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant, although no casualties have been reported. In Lebanon, the humanitarian situation is worsening, with hundreds reported killed and more than one million people displaced. UN agencies are scaling up emergency assistance despite mounting challenges. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 18 March: Lebanon displacement tops one million as nuclear safety concerns raised

events.title: Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell
events.description: Nigeria had largest increase in terrorism-related deaths, ranking fourth in global index behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger Jihadist violence rose sharply in Nigeria and Democratic Republic of Congo last year, even as global deaths from terrorism dropped to their lowest level in a decade, according to a new report. Nigeria recorded the largest increase in terrorism deaths globally in 2025, with fatalities rising by 46% from 513 in 2024 to 750, placing it fourth in the Global Terrorism Index, behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell

events.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
events.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson

events.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
events.description: As other Asian economies race to conserve energy, China has huge reserves of oil and gas as well as alternative energy sources like wind and solar Middle East crisis – live updates Xi Jinping has been preparing for a crisis like this for years. China must secure its energy supply “in its own hands”, its president was reported to have said during a visit to one of its vast oilfields in 2021. The US-Israel war on Iran plunged the Middle East into a deep conflict, with the strait of Hormuz – one of the most important waterways in global trade – all but closed and key energy facilities across the region under attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counterstrikes reported across the region as the humanitarian toll rises. UN agencies warn that health systems are under growing strain, children are increasingly affected by violence and displacement, and hundreds of thousands have been forced from their homes - especially in Lebanon. Despite mounting logistical and security challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region

events.title: Trump mocks Japan about Pearl Harbor in response to question about Iran war
events.description: US president was meeting with Japanese PM when he said: ‘Who knows better about surprise than Japan?’ Middle East crisis – live updates Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox It would be funny if it wasn’t so Trumpy. Hosting the Japanese prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , in the Oval Office on Thursday, Donald Trump could not resist mocking Japan about its 1941 attack on Pearl Harbor during the second world war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump mocks Japan about Pearl Harbor in response to question about Iran war

events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Deadly drone strikes in Sudan, families killed in West Bank, Ukraine casualties update

events.title: Deadly blasts at market and hospital raise fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency in Nigeria

events.title: Gas prices soar as energy infrastructure is attacked in the Gulf
events.description: Natural gas prices soared as attacks intensified on energy infrastructure in the Middle East. The Ras Laffan plant in Qatar has sustained "significant damage", according to its operators, which could take years to fully repair. That will have a significant impact on global supplies, because the facility produces around a fifth of the world's LNG (liquefied natural gas). Also in the show: the World Trade Organization warns a trade slowdown could lead to mounting food insecurity.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gas prices soar as energy infrastructure is attacked in the Gulf

events.title: Gas giants warn against windfall gains tax as Pocock says ‘wartime profits’ should go to struggling Australians
events.description: Government faces political fight as industry says mooted 25% levy on exports would hurt Australia’s economy and energy security Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gas giants will lobby against any federal government moves to introduce a 25% export levy on windfall profits, as crossbenchers pressure the prime minister to redirect billions of dollars in “wartime profits” to Australians struggling amid the global energy crisis. It comes after the prime minister’s department asked Treasury to model the effects of placing a flat 25% tax on gas exports, the ABC reported on Friday , along with any further changes to the petroleum resource rent tax (PRRT) and corporate income tax. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gas giants warn against windfall gains tax as Pocock says ‘wartime profits’ should go to struggling Australians

events.description: Dan Marks, a research fellow in energy security at the Royal United Services Institute, breaks down the impact of Israel's attack on Iran's massive South Pars natural gas field on global energy markets – and just how both sides could escalate the crisis with further attacks on critical energy infrastructure across the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'There's a lot of ways that this could escalate and get worse': Iran war rattles energy markets

According to Dr Shelest, Ukrainian forces demonstrate that with adaptive strategy, technological innovation and timely support, it is possible not only to resist but to go on the offensive. Yet this capacity is inseparable from sustained international backing, both military and financial.

events.title: Canada in push for joint G7 and Middle East effort to de-escalate Iran war
events.description: Foreign minister Anita Anand says she has drafted principles to reduce risk of regional spillover and wider shocks Middle East crisis – live updates Canada is pushing for a collective G7 and Middle East approach to de-escalating the Iran war, including off ramps that could bring an end to the conflict, the Canadian foreign minister, Anita Anand, has said. In London to meet the UK foreign secretary, Yvette Cooper, after talks with her Turkish counterpart, Hakan Fidan, Anand told the Guardian she hoped a G7 meeting chaired by France, this year’s president of the group, might start to build a broader collective approach to the crisis. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada in push for joint G7 and Middle East effort to de-escalate Iran war

events.title: Work from home and slow down on the road: world’s energy watchdog advises emergency measures as oil prices rise
events.description: IEA makes 10 recommendations to help households and businesses prepare for a drawn-out disruption to energy markets The world’s energy watchdog has advised governments to reduce highway speeds and encouraged workers to carpool or, ideally, work from home to combat soaring oil prices and impending fuel shortages caused by the Middle East conflict. It has also recommended countries consider limiting car access to designated zones in large cities, by giving vehicles with odd-numbered plates access on different weekdays to those with even-numbered plates. Work from home where possible to save petrol. Reduce highway speed limits by at least 10km/h to reduce fuel usage. Encourage public transport to reduce oil demand. Limit car access to roads in large cities through a number-plate rotation scheme. Increase car sharing. Encourage efficient driving for commercial vehicles through load optimisation and vehicle maintenance. Divert LPG use from transport to preserve it for essential needs like cooking. Avoid air travel where possible. Encourage electric cooking and other options to reduce reliance on LPG. Help industrial facilities switch between different petrochemical feedstocks to free up LPG. Continue reading...

events.description: An attack on Iran's key South Pars gas field has rattled global energy markets, raised fears of further escalation and driven oil prices even higher.

events.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'decimated,' Tehran claims only used ‘fraction’ of firepower
events.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel and the United States were "winning" the war against Iran, with the Islamic republic "decimated" and unable to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. However, Tehran is pushing back in this escalating war of words, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisting that Iran's military strength remains fully intact and will not faulter in the event of further attacks.

events.title: Amid deepening crisis in Palestine, girls face rising risks and a mounting mental health emergency
events.description: More than two years of unrelenting violence, displacement and loss have pushed children and young people in Palestine into what one UN official describes as a “profound mental health emergency”, with girls facing heightened risks, including a resurgence in child marriage.

events.title: ‘Hope’ for Haiti’s political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections
events.description: Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers “a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people”, according to the UN’s most senior official in the Caribbean country.

events.title: Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed

events.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as attacks on oil infrastructure continue

France 24's regional correspondent Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha, Qatar.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as attacks on oil infrastructure continue

events.title: 'Volatile: Oil market much more resilient than in 1970s' as conflict speculation drives price swings
events.description: Peter O'Brien is pleased to welcome Dan Marks, Research Fellow in energy security at the Royal United Services Institute. He views the global oil system as significantly more resilient than in past crises, in stark contrast to the 1970's. The current oil price movements being driven less by immediate physical disruptions and more by evolving expectations around the trajectory of conflict. While recent signals from political actors have temporarily eased fears of direct strikes on key facilities, volatility remains rooted in uncertainty rather than material scarcity.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Volatile: Oil market much more resilient than in 1970s' as conflict speculation drives price swings

events.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates

Trump said the reason was surprise, before asking why Japan hadn’t warned of the attack on Pearl Harbor.

events.title: Reliant on imported fuel, Pacific islands appeal for help as oil prices surge
events.description: Samoa and Tonga raise supply concerns with foreign partners as businesses and residents in Papua New Guinea grapple with higher fuel prices amid the Iran war The leaders of some Pacific countries have appealed for help with oil supplies while others urge against “panic buying” as the import-reliant nations grapple with fears over possible fuel shortages and escalating costs caused by war in the Middle East. Oil prices have surged to nearly $110 a barrel after strikes against energy infrastructure in Iran and the Gulf states. Continue reading...

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle barrels west after ripping off roofs and downing trees in far north Queensland
events.description: Second landfall expected over weekend in NT as Queensland premier says relatively limited damage so far ‘an incredibly good news story’ Live updates: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle – latest news Cyclone Narelle path tracking map How did global heating fuel Tropical Cyclone Narelle? Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakened in intensity on Friday evening after barrelling into far north Queensland as one of the state’s fiercest cyclones in living memory – downing trees, ripping off roofs and swelling rivers. As of 4pm local time, the Bureau of Meteorology downgraded Narelle from a category 3 to category 2 storm, meaning while it was less severe there were still destructive winds near the centre of 100 km/h and wind gusts up to 150 km/h. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tropical Cyclone Narelle barrels west after ripping off roofs and downing trees in far north Queensland

events.title: Outrage among EU leaders as Hungary's Orban U-turns on Ukraine loan

events.title: Some of the world’s poorest countries to lose UK aid due to 56% budget cut
events.description: UK’s bilateral aid to African countries, which funds areas such as schools and clinics, to be cut by almost £900m by 2028-29 Some of the world’s poorest countries will lose out on UK aid that funds programmes such as schools and clinics, due to budget cuts set out by the foreign secretary. The UK’s bilateral aid to African countries will be reduced by almost £900m by 2028-29 – a 56% cut – as part of more than £6bn in cuts which are funding an increase in defence spending. Continue reading...

events.title: Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader
events.description: Family of then PM, Patrice Lumumba, welcome decision to charge Étienne Davignon as ‘beginning of a reckoning’ A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled. Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes. The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga. The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men. Depriving them of a fair trial. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader

A judge-led independent committee will investigate whether fire safety standards were inadequate, if construction practices contributed to the fire, and if there were failures on the part of government officers or contractors. events.title: Uber to increase fares – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Gas giants warn against windfall gains tax as Pocock says ‘wartime profits’ should go to struggling Australians Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Far north residents in the path of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle say they have taken shelter as winds begin to swirl in the remote Cape York Communities. Sara Watkins, the owner of Coen Mechanical and the Little Bush Pantry in the township of Coen – population about 330 – say they moved to a more secure brick building when the winds picked up about 4am, local time. The wind has really started to pick up, you can hear a couple of things moving around outside. Until the wind started it was so still. It was raining but it was really still. That’s not like Coen, when it rains it pours and the wind moves about. In Coen there are a lot of old properties that have been through cyclones in the past, they are standing but they’re not cyclone rated by any means. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Uber to increase fares – as it happened

events.title: Madagascar’s military ruler decrees that ministers must pass lie detector tests

Michael Randrianirina came to power in a coup in October after weeks of youth-led protests under the banner “Gen Z Madagascar”. However, young people were quickly disenchanted by his choice of government officials, which they saw as being part of the old, corrupt elite. Continue reading...

events.title: Lyon crash out of Europa League

After seven consecutive matches without a win, Lyon now turn their focus to Ligue 1.

events.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

events.title: Long before Trump: How US policy has harmed the environment for decades
events.description: Environment advocacy groups are suing the US government over climate change policy rollback.

events.title: Eid under siege: Little to celebrate in Gaza as Israel tightens chokehold
events.description: Tighter restrictions on goods and aid are driving shortages, adding to the strain on families in Gaza on Eid.

events.title: Japan vs Australia: Women’s Asian Cup final – team news, start and lineups
events.description: Hosts Australia will be hoping to turn the tables on Japan as they meet for a third time in the Asian Cup final.

events.title: Luka Doncic scores 60 points as Lakers rally to defeat Heat in NBA
events.description: The NBA's leading scorer recorded the second 60-point game of his career as the Lakers take down the Heat in Miami.

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Delcy Rodríguez replaces Venezuela’s top military commanders
events.description: Interim president announces changes after firing defence minister, who was close to Maduro, the leader ousted by US Venezuela’s interim president has said she has replaced all her senior military commanders, the latest in a flurry of changes since the US ousted Nicolás Maduro . Delcy Rodríguez announced the changes in a social media post a day after firing the long-serving defence minister, who had been close to Maduro, and replacing him with a former intelligence chief. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas a formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
events.description: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.

events.title: Amazônia's Indigenous peoples dismantle Western cliches
events.description: Indigenous peoples and the Amazon rainsforest have often been exoticized. A new exhibition challenges these long-held tropes.

events.title: Norwegian princess says she was 'manipulated' by Epstein
events.description: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit gave an interview on her ties to the late US sex offender after new details came to light in the "Epstein files."

events.title: K-Pop stars BTS release new album ahead of comeback concert
events.description: The BTS comeback concert, to be held at Seoul's historic Gwanghwamun Square, is expected to draw around 260,000 people. The event will be livestreamed by Netflix in around 190 countries.

events.title: US: Trump image approved for commemorative gold coin
events.description: An arts panel appointed by Donald Trump backed plans for a 24-karat commemorative coin featuring the US president, drawing criticism from Democrats.

events.title: Belarus releases 250 political prisoners as part of US deal
events.description: Belarus freed 250 political prisoners in its largest release yet — a move celebrated as a humanitarian breakthrough and tied to the easing of US sanctions. However, many remain behind bars.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Belarus releases 250 political prisoners as part of US deal

events.title: EU summit: Hungary holds Ukraine aid ransom over Druzhba oil
events.description: Hungary and Slovakia have said they will not approve a major loan package for Ukraine until oil deliveries from Russia through the Druzhba pipeline are restored. EU leaders failed to sway them in Brussels.

events.title: Germany considers windfall tax amid rising fuel prices
events.description: Germany is set to pass legislation to put the brakes on rapidly rising gas prices. Berlin says a tax on petroleum profiteers is under consideration, but adds that it's too early for any drastic action.

events.title: Migrants drown after being dropped by smugglers off Comoros
events.description: At least 18 people drowned while trying to reach the French island of Mayotte. Many others were stranded hundreds of kilometers short of their destination.

events.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
events.description: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.

events.title: Venezuela interim president replaces top military commanders in sweeping reforms
events.description: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Thursday she had replaced all senior military commanders, expanding sweeping reforms after the US ousted Nicolas Maduro. The move follows the dismissal of a long-serving defence minister and the appointment of a former intelligence chief, as Caracas reshapes its armed forces.

events.title: French Socialists split over alliance with hard-left party in mayoral runoffs
events.description: Several candidates from France’s mainstream left have joined forces with Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s France Unbowed to boost their chances in mayoral runoffs on Sunday, even as others reject any association with the hard-left party, exposing a deepening divide in the French left ahead of next year's presidential election.

events.title: China not planning to invade Taiwan in 2027, US says
events.description: China is not currently planning to invade Taiwan next year, according to US spy agencies.

events.title: Social media blamed for stark decline in young people's happiness
events.description: Heavy social media use has led to a stark drop in happiness among young people, according to a major academic study.

events.title: Iranians out in strength as thousands attend funeral for security chief and navy crew
events.description: As Iran's government buried its fallen heroes, its supporters came out in strength.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iranians out in strength as thousands attend funeral for security chief and navy crew

events.title: Senegal vows to appeal 'unfair, unacceptable' ruling stripping it of African football trophy
events.description: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.

events.title: A real possum appears among plush toy animals in Australian airport gift shop
events.description: On shelves crammed with cuddly toy native animals in an Australian airport gift shop, one fluffy possum stood out because its brown eyes were moving

events.title: Congo and Rwanda agree on steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo
events.description: Congolese and Rwandan officials have agreed on coordinated steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo where Congo is fighting rebels backed by the neighboring Rwanda

events.title: Madagascar's president orders lie detector tests for candidates applying to be government ministers
events.description: Madagascar's military leader says anyone applying to become a minister in his new government will be subjected to lie detector tests to root out those who are corrupt

events.title: EU leaders slam Orbán over Ukraine loan veto, accuse election games
events.description: EU leaders are accusing Hungary’s Viktor Orbán of blocking major aid to Ukraine to score points at home ahead of elections next month

events.title: Guterres urges action against ‘rising tide of anti-Muslim hatred’
events.description: The UN chief on Monday urged countries to “work together” and eradicate a rising tide of anti-Muslim hate, calling for a rejection of “the narratives of fear and exclusion”.

events.title: Cop’s skull smashed in creek fight as repeat offender suspect walks on $100 bond
events.description: A Colorado police officer suffered a serious head injury in a violent struggle with an accused drug dealer, but the suspect was later released on a bond of just $100.

events.title: Feds charge 3 in $2.5B scheme to smuggle us AI tech to China using dummy servers
events.description: Three men linked to Supermicro are accused of smuggling $2.5 billion in AI technology to China via fake documents and shell companies, prosecutors allege.

events.title: Indiana University philanthropy group allegedly led fundraising training with Hamas-linked 'sham charity'
events.description: Indiana University's Muslim Philanthropy Initiative allegedly partnered with Hayat Yolu, a Treasury-sanctioned group accused of funding Hamas.

events.title: DHS touts 10 straight months of zero illegal aliens released at border as crossings plunge
events.description: DHS says Border Patrol recorded 10 straight months of zero migrant releases, crediting enforcement-first policies and historically low illegal crossings.

events.title: Mamdani moves to sideline NYC police with new safety office under sweeping overhaul
events.description: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced the new Office of Community Safety Thursday, signaling a shift in a public safety approach from policing to prevention and support programs.

events.title: Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez replaces sanctioned loyalist defense minister with military intel head
events.description: Acting president Delcy Rodriguez has appointed Gustavo Gonzalez Lopez to lead the defense ministry, a move to consolidate military control.
